mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month. This mechanism operates continuously regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and industrial production indicates broad-based economic acceleration, which expands profit margins and drives equity outperformance over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 50% of co-occurring growth episodes over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production relative to capacity constraints signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive or neutral during consecutive quarters of declining INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect increased consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically precedes positive equity market performance over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 45% of months with rising HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: An abrupt increase in the federal funds rate by more than 0.25 percentage points within a three-month window signals aggressive tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in more than 55% of instances following a 0.25pp rate hike within three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in employment and consumer credit balances indicates robust aggregate demand fueled by both income expansion and leverage, driving strong equity outperformance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative or flat 60-day forward return in over 50% of co-occurring growth periods across two full business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth combined with rising unemployment signals a demand-driven recession, which triggers earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of quarters where GDP growth drops below 1.0% annualized.
